attributes.description: Alerts when frontend `haproxy.requests.errors` increases by more than 10 in 5 minutes. Request errors are malformed or timed-out client requests.
attributes.name: [HAProxy OTel] Request errors spike

investigation_guide.blob:
## HAProxy request errors spike

### What fired
Counter `haproxy.requests.errors` increased by > 10 during the window.

### Why it matters
Request errors include bad HTTP framing, too-long headers, and client timeouts. A sudden spike usually reflects a faulty client, bot traffic, or an MTU/proxy misconfiguration.

### Triage
1. Inspect HAProxy access logs for the dominant error pattern.
2. Correlate with source IPs if possible.

### Remediation
- Block abusive clients at the edge.
- Fix misbehaving upstream MTU / proxy.

### Tuning
- 10 errors/5m is a generic threshold; raise for busy edges.

esqlQuery.esql:
TS metrics-haproxyreceiver.otel-*
// Request errors (malformed, timed out) — frontend-level counter
| WHERE haproxy.requests.errors IS NOT NULL
| STATS err_increase = SUM(INCREASE(haproxy.requests.errors))
  BY haproxy.proxy_name, haproxy.addr
| WHERE err_increase > 10
| EVAL alert_key = CONCAT(TO_STRING(haproxy.proxy_name), "::", COALESCE(TO_STRING(haproxy.addr), ""))
| SORT err_increase DESC
| LIMIT 50
